title: The Storm Collection Cistern

features.name: the collection pool
features.desc-on-touch: Every raindrop that fell upstream has come here. The water knows itself now—its temperature, its timing, its origin. The cistern holds the archive's wettest memory.

features.name: the fence-post markers
features.desc-on-touch: Standing like measurement points. The town planted these posts to track how much rain fell and when it fell and how it remembered falling. The archive measures itself by wetness.

features.name: the overflow pipe
features.desc-on-touch: When the cistern knows too much, it speaks through here. The town's secrets drain away as water. The plumbing has learned to gossip.

features.name: the sediment layer
features.desc-on-touch: At the bottom, where all the rain has settled into itself. Soil, memory, time—stratified. The cistern reads its own history in what has gathered here.

features.name: the rain's echo
features.desc-on-touch: The sound that the water makes when it recognizes itself. All the drops that have ever fallen here return as a single note of belonging.

features.name: the cistern's skin
features.desc-on-touch: Weathered stone, sealed by algae and memory. The boundary between the town's plumbing and the archive's wetness. It knows what flows through it and what remains.

inscription: where all the region's rain has come to know itself, the cistern holds its own deep memory and the archive counts the ways it has been wet

file.name: Stormwater_Convergence_Calibration_LogMMYYYY.csv
file.note: Record of every rainstorm's path through the civic plumbing. Each entry marked with the precise moment the water realized it had come home.

exits.label: back upstream through the branching ducts
toward the storm's beginning, where rain first falls and hasn't yet learned to gather
deeper into the cistern, where the water's knowing becomes darker
